data.public_job_desc:
Position: AI Architect

Location: Fort Worth, TX (Hybrid – 3 days onsite)

Rate : $70/hr on C2C

Duration: 6 Months+

 

Job Description:

  • Master’s/PhD degree with 3+ years of experience in quantitative discipline (e.g., Data Science, Machine Learning Engineer, Computer Science, Applied Mathematics, Statistics, etc.)

  • Experience with Python programming language

  • Practical experience with data extraction, cleaning, and analysis

  • Depth of knowledge in statistical and machine learning techniques Preferred qualifications – Education & Prior Job Experience

  • 10+ years of experience in a technical professional environment, in addition to the minimum requirements

  • Strong understanding and hands on -building traditional ML model (classification, Regression and all varities of Supervised Models)

  • Strong hands on Deep Learning Algorithm and implementations

  • Advance AIML knowledge

  • Must have deployed the end to end Machine Learning pipeline.

  • Practical experience designing, building and deploying machine learning models

  • Experience in using cloud platforms and parallel processing to scale model development/ deployment (Databricks, Azure)

  • Domain knowledge in the airline industry

  • Experience working in a consulting role

  • Experience with SQL and data visualization (Tableau, PowerBI)

  • Platform mindset + reuse — building shared components/templates/patterns used by multiple teams (not one off implementations).

  • Operational readiness for AI — monitoring/telemetry, production maintenance practices, and reliability-focused troubleshooting.

  • AI Platform architecture, API design, distributed systems, reliability engineering- building reusable platform components used by multiple teams (not one off apps)

  • Experience implementing AI quality/evaluation practices and AI telemetry/monitoring foundations.

  • Ability to effectively communicate both verbally and written with all levels within the organization

  • Demonstrated motivation and aptitude for logical analysis, problem identification and problem solving

  • Ability to view data from different angles to employ feature engineering techniques to better represent models

  • Ability to work on a diverse team with diverse skillsets

  • Must Have Skills: (with MS/Phd) Python, Machine Learning, Deep Learning , ML design and deployment

Job Title: Automation Analyst – QE
Location: Mt. Laurel, NJ (Local Candidates Only)
Experience Required: 6–8 Years
 

Job Summary

We are seeking an experienced Automation Analyst – QE with strong expertise in Selenium-based automation testing and a solid understanding of API, ETL, and database testing. The ideal candidate should have experience working in Agile environments and exposure to modern testing tools and CI/CD practices.

Required Skills

  • Selenium Automation Testing
  • Automation Testing
  • Test Planning
  • REST API Testing
  • SQL & Data Validation
  • Playwright (Hands-on experience or knowledge)
  • ETL Testing Concepts
  • GitHub Copilot
  • CI/CD Practices
  • Agile Methodology
  • Strong Analytical & Problem-Solving Skills

Preferred Skills

  • Java
  • Spring Framework
  • Selenium
  • REST Services
  • API Testing
  • Test Planning
  • Automation Testing

Roles & Responsibilities

  • Develop and execute automated test scripts using Selenium and/or Playwright.
  • Perform API testing and validate REST services.
  • Conduct SQL queries and data validation activities.
  • Support ETL testing and data quality validation.
  • Participate in test planning, test execution, and defect tracking.
  • Collaborate with development and QA teams in Agile environments.
  • Utilize CI/CD pipelines and GitHub Copilot to improve testing efficiency.
  • Analyze test results and provide actionable feedback to stakeholders.

data.public_job_desc:
Hi
Hope you are doing good.!
Position: Programmer/Developer
Location: 505 Hudson Street   Hartford ,  Connecticut  -Hybrid

Job Summary:
Please note that the first and second round interviews will be held IN PERSON on Thursdays from 9-4pm.
Please do not submit a contractor who can not make this
-------
 
STATE OF CONNECTICUT 
Department of Children and Families 
Statement of Work For CCIWS Program  
Developer/Salesforce Developer
  
===========================================
 (X) New Task    () Renewal       ( ) Fixed Price               (X) On Site Support       (X) Remote Support
===========================================
1.  REQUIRED AGENCY:
Department of Children and Families
DCF IS Division
505 Hudson Street
Hartford, CT 06106       
2.  PROJECT/SYSTEM/PROGRAM OVERVIEW
The Department of Children and Families depends on the core, mission-critical applications, CT-KIND and IV-E, Connecticut’s Federally-reimbursed SACWIS (Statewide Automated Child Welfare Information System).  Implemented in 1996, available on every desktop, and used by over 2,000 social workers and case managers, the application has been enhanced and modified continually to meet data capture and process functionality requirements driven by good child welfare case practice as well as State and Federal legislation and statute.  Additionally, LINK continues to experience change chiefly in compliance with the 1991 Juan F. Consent Decree as well as the agency’s ongoing goal of improving the safety, permanency and well-being of children in care.  The agency is currently committed to developing the next generation Comprehensive Child Welfare information system (CCWIS) and has invested significant resources to ensure that Connecticut has efficient, economic and effective tools to aid our Child Welfare Staff, Providers and the children and families of CT. 
DCF Information Systems provides technology expertise and services as well as an information systems infrastructure to the agency and its roughly 3,300 employees.  DCF Information Systems strives to improve continually improving technology services that are cost-effective and of the highest effectiveness and quality.
 
3.  SCOPE OF WORK
The agency needs the service of a senior level Full Stack Developer. The candidate will serve in the capacity of a Developer participating in various SDLC processes including requirements verification, design and development, testing, maintenance, and support activities per the direction of agency management. The specific focus of this candidate will be the to be part of the team which supports existing custom Salesforce applications as well as to participates in the SDLC process of the new CT-KIND system and work with the System Integrator to ensure the transfer of knowledge and on-going maintenance of the solution for a given module.   The candidate must have a minimum of 5 years of Salesforce development. Must know foundational OOP in apex.
 
Specific Services Required:
In the role of the Salesforce Developer, the following services are required:
·        Must be very organized, pro-active and self-directed.
·        Must be self-starter and driven.  Must Drive the work from requirements to implementation.
·        Must have very strong abstract thinking and problem-solving skills.
·        Must have very good verbal and written communication skills.
·        Design and development Salesforce APEX, Lightning pages, Visual Force Pages
·        Full stack. Salesforce, OOP, CRUD, Design patterns, Configuration SOQL
·        Must have advanced knowledge of Salesforce development (OOP, Design Patterns)
Bd  Provide analysis/requirements to integrate applications.
·        Work on all phases of SDLC and create/maintain required technical documentation.
·     
·        .
 
 
 
REQUIRED SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE
 
Refer to the State contracts for IT Professional Services Agreement (22PSX0163) for experience and skill requirements for the job classification Solution Architect to fill the role.  In addition, the agency requires:
 
For this position, the candidate shares experience and judgment with team to plan and accomplish goals, mentors less experienced staff, a wide degree of creativity and latitude is expected. In addition, the agency requires:
 
An experience professional with 10 or more years of prior experience demonstrating the following knowledge and skills:
      Salesforce (min: 5 years) Must be able to code APEX OOP and know design patterns.
·               .NET OOP and SOA
            JavaScript Frameworks preferred
             SQL
             End-to-End Web Development
 
 
 
 
 
4. ADMINISTRATIVE CONSIDERATIONS
 
 
Work Schedule: Full-Time 40hrs - Monday - Friday, 8 AM - 5 PM
 
 
State Resources and Oversight: DCF IS Management
 
 
Security/Privacy Considerations: Comply with Vendor Qualification, DCF Child Protection Background Check and DCF Non-disclosure Agreement
 
General/Miscellaneous:

DCF Information Systems provides technology expertise and services as well as an information systems infrastructure to the agency and its roughly 3,300 employees.  DCF Information Systems strives to improve continually improving technology services that are cost-effective and of the highest effectiveness and quality.
 
3.  SCOPE OF WORK
The agency needs the service of a senior level Full Stack Developer. The candidate will serve in the capacity of a Developer participating in various SDLC processes including requirements verification, design and development, testing, maintenance, and support activities per the direction of agency management. The specific focus of this candidate will be the to be part of the team which supports existing custom Salesforce applications as well as to participates in the SDLC process of the new CT-KIND system and work with the System Integrator to ensure the transfer of knowledge and on-going maintenance of the solution for a given module.   The candidate must have a minimum of 5 years of Salesforce development. Must know foundational OOP in apex.
 
Specific Services Required:
In the role of the Salesforce Developer, the following services are required:
·        Must be very organized, pro-active and self-directed.
·        Must be self-starter and driven.  Must Drive the work from requirements to implementation.
·        Must have very strong abstract thinking and problem-solving skills.
·        Must have very good verbal and written communication skills.
·        Design and development Salesforce APEX, Lightning pages, Visual Force Pages
·        Full stack. Salesforce, OOP, CRUD, Design patterns, Configuration SOQL
·        Must have advanced knowledge of Salesforce development (OOP, Design Patterns)
Bd  Provide analysis/requirements to integrate applications.
·        Work on all phases of SDLC and create/maintain required technical documentation.
·     
·        .
 
 
 
REQUIRED SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE
 
Refer to the State contracts for IT Professional Services Agreement (22PSX0163) for experience and skill requirements for the job classification Solution Architect to fill the role.  In addition, the agency requires:
 
For this position, the candidate shares experience and judgment with team to plan and accomplish goals, mentors less experienced staff, a wide degree of creativity and latitude is expected. In addition, the agency requires:
 
An experience professional with 10 or more years of prior experience demonstrating the following knowledge and skills:
      Salesforce (min: 5 years) Must be able to code APEX OOP and know design patterns.
·               .NET OOP and SOA
            JavaScript Frameworks preferred
             SQL
             End-to-End Web Development
 
 
 
 
 
4. ADMINISTRATIVE CONSIDERATIONS
 
 
Work Schedule: Full-Time 40hrs - Monday - Friday, 8 AM - 5 PM
 
 
State Resources and Oversight: DCF IS Management
 
 
Security/Privacy Considerations: Comply with Vendor Qualification, DCF Child Protection Background Check and DCF Non-disclosure Agreement
 
General/Miscellaneous:

data.public_job_desc:
Requirement name : Application Support Analyst
Location :450 Columbus Blvd   Hartford,  Connecticut-Hybrid
 
Job Summary:
Purpose:
Ther Office of Early Childhood is seeking a Application Support Analyst to provide end-user support, assist technical analysts, and support ongoing enhancements to our current portals built on the Salesforce platform. This role will also support the delivery and rollout of a new Single-Entry Portal, working closely with business stakeholders, developers, analysts, and agile teams.   This is a hybrid position as the incumbent will be required to be onsite at least one day a week.
Scope of Work:
The Contractor will provide day-to-day technical support for end users of the Salesforce-based portals, troubleshoot issues, validate enhancements, and assist in feature rollout activities. Additionally, the Contractor will support technical analysts with requirement clarifications, testing, small configuration updates, communication with stakeholders, and documentation. The Contractor will also assist in the adoption and support of the upcoming Single-Entry Portal, including user onboarding, issue intake, root cause identification, and coordination with the technical team for resolution.
Deliverables:
- End-user technical support responses and resolutions
- Issue triage, documentation, and tracking in Azure DevOps and JIRA
- Support tickets with troubleshooting notes
- Feedback documentation for enhancements and new portal features
- UAT support for Salesforce and Single Entry Portal releases
- User support guides, knowledge base entries, or process documentation as needed
Work will be ongoing throughout the engagement, supporting bi-weekly or monthly release cycles. Major activities include onboarding, portal enhancement support, UAT cycles, user feedback gathering, and Single Entry Portal rollout support.
Successful fulfillment of support responsibilities, timely response to user-reported issues, accurate documentation, effective communication with technical analysts, and positive feedback from business stakeholders. Contractor should demonstrate deep understanding of the Salesforce provider and parent portals and provide high-quality end-user support.
Responsibilities:
- Provide technical support to end users of the provider portal, parent portal, and Single Entry Portal
- Assist technical analysts with enhancements, testing, and documentation
- Participate in agile ceremonies, backlog refinement, and release readiness discussions
- Perform root cause analysis and collaborate with developers on bug resolution
- Support UAT test cycles and portal validation activities
- Maintain clear communication with stakeholders and users

data.public_job_desc:
Job Title: AWS Cloud Developer
Location: Sacramento, CA (Hybrid)
End Client: CalSTRS – California Teachers Retirement System
Duration: Long Term
Visa: Except OPT & CPT
Experience Required: 10+ Years

Job Description

We are looking for an experienced AWS Cloud Developer with strong expertise in cloud infrastructure, application development, CI/CD automation, and cloud security. The ideal candidate should have hands-on experience in designing, developing, and deploying scalable cloud-native applications on AWS.

Required Skills:

  • Strong experience with AWS Cloud Platform – EC2, S3, Lambda, IAM
  • Experience with CI/CD pipelines, GitHub, and version control workflows
  • Hands-on expertise in Infrastructure as Code (Terraform, CloudFormation, or ARM)
  • Strong development experience with REST APIs and web applications using Python, Node.js, or C#
  • Experience with Containers and Orchestration – Docker, Kubernetes (EKS)
  • Knowledge of SQL and NoSQL databases – MySQL, PostgreSQL, MongoDB, DynamoDB
  • Experience with Security & Compliance standards including encryption, Secrets Manager/Vault, HIPAA, GDPR, and SOC2
  • Strong troubleshooting, deployment, and cloud automation skills

Responsibilities:

  • Design, develop, and maintain scalable AWS cloud solutions
  • Implement CI/CD pipelines and automate deployments
  • Develop and manage Infrastructure as Code solutions
  • Build and maintain secure REST APIs and cloud-native applications
  • Manage containerized applications using Docker and Kubernetes
  • Ensure cloud security, compliance, and best practices
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams for cloud migration and modernization initiatives

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Experience working in Government/Public Sector projects is a plus
  • AWS Certifications preferred
  • Strong communication and problem-solving skills
data.skills: AWS Cloud – EC2, S3, Lambda, IAM, Terraform / CloudFormation, CI/CD Pipelines & GitHub, Python / Node.js / C#, Docker & Kubernetes (EKS)

data.public_job_desc:
Position Title: GDPR Compliance Readiness & Assessment Consultant

Client: Chicago Bridge and Iron
Employment Type: Contract
Work Location: Hybrid / Remote / Onsite as Required
Status: Accepting Candidates

Job Description

We are seeking an experienced GDPR Compliance Readiness & Assessment Consultant to support enterprise-wide data privacy and regulatory compliance initiatives aligned with the European Union’s General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR). The consultant will evaluate organizational readiness, identify compliance gaps, assess business processes and technologies handling personal data, and provide strategic remediation recommendations across IT, Security, Legal, and Operations teams.

The ideal candidate will have strong expertise in privacy regulations, cybersecurity controls, enterprise governance frameworks, and risk management methodologies, along with excellent communication skills for engaging both technical and executive stakeholders.

Key Responsibilities

  • Conduct GDPR readiness assessments across enterprise applications, infrastructure, cloud environments, and business processes
  • Evaluate organizational handling of Personally Identifiable Information (PII) and sensitive data
  • Perform data flow mapping and data inventory assessments
  • Assess compliance with GDPR principles including:
    • Data minimization
    • Right to access
    • Right to be forgotten
    • Consent management
    • Data retention
    • Privacy by design
  • Review security controls related to:
    • Identity & Access Management (IAM)
    • Encryption
    • Logging & Monitoring
    • Incident Response
    • Data Loss Prevention (DLP)
  • Conduct gap analyses against GDPR regulatory requirements and industry frameworks
  • Develop remediation roadmaps and compliance improvement plans
  • Collaborate with Legal, Security, Infrastructure, Application, Cloud, and Business teams
  • Review third-party/vendor data processing agreements and associated risk exposure
  • Assist with Data Protection Impact Assessments (DPIAs)
  • Support audit preparation and regulatory response activities
  • Prepare executive-level assessment reports, findings documentation, and risk summaries
  • Recommend governance processes, policies, and operational controls
  • Support implementation of privacy and compliance tools/technologies

Required Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Cybersecurity, Information Systems, Computer Science, Risk Management, or related field
  • 5+ years of experience in:
    • GDPR Compliance
    • Security Assessments
    • IT Governance
    • Risk & Compliance
    • Privacy Consulting
  • Strong understanding of:
    • GDPR regulations
    • Data privacy frameworks
    • Information security controls
    • Enterprise IT environments
  • Experience conducting compliance assessments and readiness reviews
  • Hands-on knowledge of cloud platforms:
    • AWS
    • Azure
    • Google Cloud Platform (GCP)
  • Familiarity with enterprise applications:
    • SAP
    • Salesforce
    • Microsoft 365
    • ServiceNow
    • ERP/CRM platforms
  • Experience with compliance frameworks:
    • ISO 27001
    • NIST
    • SOC 2
    • HIPAA
    • CCPA
  • Strong documentation and executive presentation skills

Preferred Certifications

  • CIPP/E – Certified Information Privacy Professional (Europe)
  • CIPM – Certified Information Privacy Manager

Skills Matrix

Category

Skills

Privacy & Compliance

GDPR, CCPA, HIPAA, DPIA, Data Privacy

Governance & Risk

IT Governance, Risk Management, Compliance Assessments

Security Controls

IAM, Encryption, DLP, Incident Response, Logging & Monitoring

Frameworks

ISO 27001, NIST, SOC 2

Cloud Platforms

AWS, Azure, Google Cloud

Enterprise Applications

SAP, Salesforce, Microsoft 365, ServiceNow

Soft Skills

Executive Reporting, Documentation, Stakeholder Management

data.public_job_desc:
Job Title: Electrical / Electronics Engineer - Hardware

Department: Research & Development

Location: Charlotte, NC 

Reports To: Director of R&D

Direct Reports: No
Duration: Long Term

 

POSITION SUMMARY

The Electrical Engineer will select or design all electrical components and Printed Circuit Assemblies (PCA) for a comprehensive line of automotive electrical diagnostic products and accessories. You will lead the development of high-speed digital circuits with embedded microcontrollers usually battery powered, precision analog front-ends to maintain signal quality, and robust power management systems from initial concept to high-volume manufacturing.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

  • PCA & Circuit Design: Design complex multi-layer PCBs and PCAs, including schematic capture and layout, using industry-standard tools like Altium Designer (preferred) or OrCAD.

  • Component Selection: Identify and source high-reliability automotive-grade components (AEC-Q or CAT qualified) for microcontrollers, sensors, wiring/ribbon and communication transceivers.

  • Communication Protocols: Integrate and validate communication interfaces, including Bluetooth, CAN, LIN, Ethernet, and OBD-II, as needed.

  • Power Management: Develop efficient power systems for battery-operated handheld devices, including charging circuits and high-voltage protection for vehicle-connected probes.

  • Signal Integrity & Testing: Conduct simulations and hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) testing to ensure diagnostic accuracy and immunity to electromagnetic interference (EMI/EMC).

  • Prototyping & Validation: Build and debug electrical prototypes, performing root-cause analysis on PCA failures and optimizing for mass production (Design for Manufacturability and Testability - DFM/DFT).

  

QUALIFICATIONS

Education/Experience Required

  • Bachelor’s degree or master’s degree in electrical or electronics Engineering, or a related field experience.

  • 3-5 years of experience in electronics hardware design, ideally focusing on handheld electronic devices or automotive equipment.

  • Design Tools: Mastery of PCB layout software and circuit simulation tools like PSpice, LTspice, or MATLAB/Simulink.

  • Embedded Systems: Strong understanding of embedded hardware architecture (STM ARM Cortex-M Family) and experience interfacing with firmware teams.

  • Instrumentation: Proficiency with advanced laboratory equipment, including high-speed oscilloscopes, spectrum analyzers, and logic analyzers.

  • Standards & Compliance: Deep knowledge of electronics safety and regulatory standards, such as CE, UL, or automotive-specific AEC-Q certifications, and IEC 61010 (Safety for measurement equipment) 

  • Experience collaborating with subcontractors and integrating supply chain partners.

  • Basic knowledge of project management methodologies and the ability to collaborate with teams to complete projects on time and within budget.

  • Strategic mindset with a focus on innovation, continuous improvement, and product development.

  • Strong decision-making and organizational skills.

  • Willingness to travel up to 10% annually as required.

 

Preferred Skills

  • Technical knowledge of diagnostic systems, electronic components, and automotive electrical systems 

  • Familiarity with automotive electrical systems and diagnostic protocols (e.g., CAN bus, ODB-II) is highly preferred.

  • General knowledge of ERP for new parts/SKU creation and Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) systems to control file revisions (Engineering Changes – ECN). 

  • Automotive industry experience is preferred but not required.

  • Bilingual English/Spanish is a plus.
data.skills: Design, PCA, PCB, Circuits, Embedded, Electronics, (Altium OR OrCAD), (PSpice OR LTspice OR Matlab OR Simulink)

Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field

Role Overview:

We are seeking a highly experienced Java Full Stack Senior Developer with strong expertise in modern Java technologies, Angular, Microservices architecture, and enterprise application development. The ideal candidate will be responsible for designing, developing, and supporting scalable multi-tier applications while leading technical initiatives, mentoring teams, and driving best development practices.

The role requires strong backend and frontend development expertise, hands-on experience with CI/CD pipelines, Agile methodologies, and large-scale enterprise systems.

Key Responsibilities:

Software Development & Architecture

  • Design, develop, and maintain enterprise-grade full stack applications
  • Build scalable backend services using Java, Spring Framework, Spring Boot, Hibernate, and JPA
  • Develop responsive frontend applications using Angular 8+/9, JavaScript, jQuery, Ajax, and Bootstrap
  • Work on Microservices-based architecture and RESTful APIs
  • Implement robust object-oriented design principles and design patterns

Technical Leadership

  • Conduct code reviews and provide technical guidance to development teams
  • Identify technical risks and implement mitigation strategies
  • Mentor team members and support technical skill development
  • Ensure coding standards, security practices, and performance optimization

Database & Messaging

  • Work extensively with large-volume databases, PL/SQL, and stored procedures
  • Manage messaging integrations using JMS, JBoss Messaging, and ActiveMQ
  • Perform data analysis and align technical solutions with business processes

DevOps & CI/CD

  • Manage source control and repository workflows using GitHub
  • Work with CI/CD tools such as Jenkins and Liquibase
  • Support deployment planning and release management activities
  • Ensure code quality using SonarQube, JUnit, and testing frameworks

Agile & Team Collaboration

  • Work within Agile/Scrum delivery environments
  • Participate in sprint planning, retrospectives, and backlog grooming
  • Collaborate with stakeholders, management, QA, and clients to ensure successful delivery
  • Track team metrics and coordinate with Jira administration teams for required customizations

Troubleshooting & Continuous Improvement

  • Identify recurring production issues and perform root cause analysis
  • Conduct troubleshooting and system performance optimization
  • Stay updated with latest technology trends and AI-assisted development tools (e.g., GitHub Copilot)

Reporting & Communication

  • Prepare project status reports and technical presentations
  • Communicate project progress, risks, and technical updates to stakeholders
  • Foster a transparent and collaborative team environment

Required Technical Skills:

  • Java (JDK 8 & JDK 21)
  • Spring Framework 3.x
  • Spring Boot
  • Hibernate 4.0
  • JPA 2.0
  • Angular 8+/9
  • JavaScript, jQuery, Ajax, Bootstrap
  • RichFaces (JSF Component Framework)
  • JBoss Application Server
  • REST Web Services
  • Microservices Architecture
  • PL/SQL & Stored Procedures
  • JMS, ActiveMQ, JBoss Messaging
  • SonarQube, JUnit
  • GitHub / Version Control
  • Jenkins, Liquibase
  • Agile/Scrum Methodology
  • UML Modeling & Design Patterns

Preferred Skills:

  • React.js knowledge
  • AI-assisted development tools (GitHub Copilot)
  • Experience in complex multi-tier enterprise applications
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
  • Excellent communication and leadership abilities

Keywords:

Java Full Stack Developer, Spring Boot, Angular 9, JDK 21, Hibernate, Microservices, REST API, JBoss, ActiveMQ, Jenkins, Liquibase, GitHub, PL/SQL, Agile, Full Stack Development, CI/CD, Enterprise Applications

data.public_job_desc:
Job Title: QA Automation Engineer / Business Analyst (Playwright)

Location: Plano, TX

Experience Required: 5–10+ Years

Education:

Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science or related field

Role Overview:

We are seeking a QA Automation Engineer / Business Analyst with strong experience in Playwright automation, QA methodologies, and end-to-end business process validation. The ideal candidate will work closely with business and technical teams to ensure high-quality delivery across multiple applications and microservices-based systems.

This role requires strong experience in both manual and automation testing, along with business analysis capabilities to translate requirements into actionable development and testing deliverables.

Key Responsibilities:

Business Analysis & Stakeholder Management

  • Engage with stakeholders across departments to gather, validate, and prioritize business requirements
  • Translate business requirements into detailed user stories and system specifications
  • Conduct impact analysis and gap assessments to support solution design
  • Define business rules and ensure alignment between business and technical teams
  • Support Agile ceremonies including sprint planning, backlog grooming, and retrospectives

QA & Testing Activities

  • Develop test plans and test strategies for multiple applications and business processes
  • Perform System Testing, Integration Testing, Regression Testing, and User Acceptance Testing (UAT)
  • Conduct UAT coordination and defect analysis/troubleshooting
  • Ensure quality across end-to-end application workflows and microservices-based systems

Automation Testing

  • Develop and execute automation test scripts using Playwright (primary)
  • Experience with Selenium or other automation tools is required
  • Maintain and enhance automation frameworks and test suites
  • Integrate automation scripts into testing lifecycle for regression and functional testing
  • Ensure stability and scalability of test automation frameworks

Functional & Technical Understanding

  • Understand microservices architecture and application workflows
  • Validate end-to-end system flows across multiple applications
  • Identify dependencies and support cross-functional resolution of issues

Quality Assurance Practices

  • Apply QA methodologies and best practices across SDLC
  • Ensure defect tracking, reporting, and resolution management
  • Maintain high-quality test documentation including manual test cases and test plans

Required Skills:

  • Strong experience in Playwright Automation Tool
  • Selenium or equivalent automation tools
  • QA Processes & Methodologies
  • Manual Testing (Test Plans, Test Cases)
  • Automation Testing Framework Maintenance
  • System, Integration, Regression Testing, and UAT
  • Business Requirement Analysis & Stakeholder Management
  • Microservices Architecture Understanding
  • Agile/Scrum Methodology
  • Defect Tracking & Troubleshooting

Preferred Skills:

  • Experience working with multiple business applications
  • Strong experience in end-to-end application workflow testing
  • Experience working in large enterprise environments
  • Strong communication and collaboration skills across teams

Top Skills:

  • Playwright Automation
  • Stakeholder Engagement & Business Analysis
  • End-to-End QA across Multiple Applications

Keywords:

Playwright, QA Automation Engineer, Selenium, Business Analyst, UAT, Regression Testing, Microservices, Test Automation Framework, Agile, Test Planning, System Testing, Integration Testing, Requirement Gathering

data.public_job_desc:
Automation Architect – AI, Cloud & Quality Engineering

LPL Financial

Austin TX [Hybrid] 


1. Automation Framework Engineering

·                     Collaborate closely with developers to understand system architecture, data flows, and event-driven behaviors for effective automation design.

  • Build and enhance end-to-end automation frameworks supporting API, integration, and data validation layers.
  • Develop integration test suites to ensure reliability and stability across development and pre-production environments.
  • Automate regression, functional, and event-driven validations using Playwright, PyTest, TestNG, and related tools.
  • Contribute to modernization initiatives transitioning from legacy to distributed systems, ensuring comprehensive automation coverage and system resilience.

2. AI & Intelligent Test Enablement

·                     Leverage tools such as GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT, and LLM-based agents to assist in automation scripting, test generation, and analysis.

  • Integrate GenAI tools for test documentation, failure triage, and predictive quality analytics.
  • Experiment with autonomous testing agents capable of self-triggering and monitoring test pipelines.
  • Drive the adoption of AI-enhanced automation strategies to improve efficiency, quality insights, and test coverage.

3. Cloud & DevOps Integration

·                     Integrate automation frameworks within CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, Jenkins, TeamCity) to enable continuous validation.

  • Deploy and manage test environments on AWS (EKS, SQS, Glue, Lambda, DynamoDB, PostgreSQL).
  • Implement observability and test telemetry to monitor pipeline health, performance, and coverage trends.
  • Collaborate with DevOps and platform engineering teams to ensure environment consistency, scalability, and release readiness.

4. Agile Collaboration & Leadership

·                     Actively participate in Agile/Scrum ceremonies—including sprint planning, daily standups, and retrospectives—to align testing and development objectives.

  • Mentor engineers in automation best practices and modern framework adoption.
  • Foster cross-functional alignment by collaborating with developers, architects, and product owners to ensure quality is engineered from the start.
  • Champion a quality-first culture by embedding continuous testing and automation throughout the delivery lifecycle.

 

Tech Stack & Tools

Languages: Java/C#/ Python, JavaScript/TypeScript
Frameworks: Playwright, Selenium, PyTest, TestNG, JUnit
Cloud & DevOps: AWS (EKS, Glue, SQS, Lambda, DynamoDB), Jenkins, GitHub Actions, Docker, Kubernetes
Databases: PostgreSQL, Redis, DynamoDB, MongoDB
AI Tools: GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT, OpenAI Assistants
Methodologies: Agile, Scrum, Domain-Driven Design, Event-Driven Architecture

Required Skills & Experience

·                     Experience in test automation and quality engineering within complex enterprise environments.

  • Strong understanding of software development workflows with proven collaboration alongside development and architecture teams.
  • Expertise in Playwright and API automation frameworks, with proficiency in Java, Python, or C#.
  • Experience testing event-driven systems and microservice-based architectures.
  • Hands-on experience with AWS cloud services and CI/CD automation practices.
  • Exposure to GenAI tools (Copilot, ChatGPT, LLM-based assistants) for enhanced automation productivity.
  • Proven experience working in Agile/Scrum environments as an active and contributing team member.

Preferred Qualifications

·                     Knowledge of AI-based automation, autonomous test agents, or intelligent QA platforms.

  • Certifications in AWS DevOps, Google Cloud, or Azure Fundamentals.
  • Experience with financial systems or data-intensive platforms.
  • Strong leadership and communication skills, with a passion for mentoring and cross-functional collaboration.

Location: Atlanta, GA

Employment Type: Contract

Job Summary

We are seeking a skilled QA Automation Tester with strong experience in manual and automation testing across web and mobile applications. The ideal candidate should have hands-on expertise in Selenium-based automation frameworks, testing tools, bug tracking systems, and Java programming. Experience in the Unemployment Insurance domain is a strong plus.

Job Title: Business Analyst (Agile Delivery Team)

Location: Houston, TX (Locals Only)

Duration: Initial backfill through late summer/early fall with strong potential for long-term extension

Rate: For citizens 80 on w2, For H1b, h4 ead, GC 80$ on c2c

Client: Constellation Energy

Visa: Any Valid Work Authorization

Job Description:

Constellation Energy is looking for a Business Analyst (BA) to support one of their Agile delivery teams. This role will initially support the Prowl team while assisting with growing project demands and ongoing Calpine-related initiatives. Strong potential for long-term extension or transition into another Genesis delivery team.

Key Responsibilities:
• Gather, analyze, and document business requirements
• Write user stories and acceptance criteria
• Work closely with Product Owners, Scrum Masters, developers, and business stakeholders
• Support Agile ceremonies and delivery processes
• Connect end-to-end business processes across teams
• Collaborate with technical and non-technical stakeholders
• Assist in operational and commercial system initiatives

Team Supports:
• Accounting
• Position Management
• Payments
• Operational Processes

Required Skills:
• Strong experience in requirements gathering and documentation
• Experience writing user stories and acceptance criteria
• Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills
• Strong understanding of Agile methodologies and ceremonies
• Ability to work in fast-paced and evolving environments
• Experience analyzing end-to-end business processes

Nice to Have:
• Experience in Energy, Retail, or Operational Systems
• Exposure to integration or transformation projects
• Experience with data analysis and reporting workflows

Additional Information:
• Immediate interview process
• Quick onboarding opportunity
• Strong collaborative Agile environment
• Excellent long-term project potential

data.position_title: Digital Engineer on site in San Diego, CA.
data.public_job_desc:
Job Title - Engineer Digital 3

Number of Positions - 1

Job Location - 15121 Innovation Drive, San Diego, California, United States of America, 92128

 

Job Description -


We are looking for you to join our team as a Digital Engineer on site in San Diego, CA.

 

Responsibilities:

  • 12 or more years of professional technical experience.
  • Hands-on technical leadership and mentoring a team of 2-4 VHDL verification engineers
  • Define and maintain digital logic design verification frameworks and architectures for software-defined radio systems
  • Develop a verification plan based on digital logic design requirements and design specifications
  • Simulate and verify FPGA logic designs, requirements, and perform system validation
  • Analyze and debug simulation failures, determine root causes of bugs, and work with designers to resolve issues
  • Perform regression testing and generate verification metrics and reporting
  • Operate in a team environment with 5-7 engineers

 

Basic Qualifications:

  • Experience in VHDL design and verification methodologies for DoD communications systems
  • FPGA deployable product process experience (requirements definition, conceptual design, detailed design, verification, and production release)
  • Experience with technical management of tasks, forecasting, and estimating schedules to ensure completion to meet schedule and quality standards
  • Demonstrated strong analytical skills and the ability to prioritize assignments according to program goals
  • Demonstrated ability to coordinate and communicate effectively with members of cross-functional teams and program management
  • Experience developing verification plans, functional tests, test benches, and bus functional models
  • Experience using Siemens Questa Simulator
  • Must be a US Citizen with the ability to obtain and maintain a Security Clearance

 

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Active Security Clearance
  • Experience with leading a team with various levels of experience and skills
  • Experience with the management of cost and metric reporting
  • Experience with functional verification methodology using OSVVM for the full life cycle of products
  • Experience with scripting languages for Verification automation in a Linux environment (Perl, TCL, Bash, and Makefile)
  • Experience using Siemens formal applications such as Lint, Inspect, and CDC
  • Ability to hold special access program clearances

data.requisition_description:
Job Title - Engineer Digital 3

Number of Positions - 1

Job Location - 15121 Innovation Drive, San Diego, California, United States of America, 92128

 

Job Description -


We are looking for you to join our team as a Digital Engineer on site in San Diego, CA.

 

Responsibilities:

  • 12 or more years of professional technical experience.
  • Hands-on technical leadership and mentoring a team of 2-4 VHDL verification engineers
  • Define and maintain digital logic design verification frameworks and architectures for software-defined radio systems
  • Develop a verification plan based on digital logic design requirements and design specifications
  • Simulate and verify FPGA logic designs, requirements, and perform system validation
  • Analyze and debug simulation failures, determine root causes of bugs, and work with designers to resolve issues
  • Perform regression testing and generate verification metrics and reporting
  • Operate in a team environment with 5-7 engineers

 

Basic Qualifications:

  • Experience in VHDL design and verification methodologies for DoD communications systems
  • FPGA deployable product process experience (requirements definition, conceptual design, detailed design, verification, and production release)
  • Experience with technical management of tasks, forecasting, and estimating schedules to ensure completion to meet schedule and quality standards
  • Demonstrated strong analytical skills and the ability to prioritize assignments according to program goals
  • Demonstrated ability to coordinate and communicate effectively with members of cross-functional teams and program management
  • Experience developing verification plans, functional tests, test benches, and bus functional models
  • Experience using Siemens Questa Simulator
  • Must be a US Citizen with the ability to obtain and maintain a Security Clearance

 

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Active Security Clearance
  • Experience with leading a team with various levels of experience and skills
  • Experience with the management of cost and metric reporting
  • Experience with functional verification methodology using OSVVM for the full life cycle of products
  • Experience with scripting languages for Verification automation in a Linux environment (Perl, TCL, Bash, and Makefile)
  • Experience using Siemens formal applications such as Lint, Inspect, and CDC
  • Ability to hold special access program clearances

data.public_job_desc:
Job Title: Azure Technical Delivery Manager
Experience: Min: 12+ years
Certifications: Minimum any one Azure Certifications (e.g., Azure Solutions Architect Expert, Azure DevOps Engineer Expert, Azure Data Engineer Associate, Azure Administrator Associate, Azure Security Engineer)
Location: King of Prussia, PA (Hybrid, 3/2)
______________
Role Overview
The Azure Technical Delivery Manager will lead end-to-end delivery of Microsoft Azure cloud solutions, ensuring alignment with business objectives and technical best practices. This role combines deep cloud infrastructure and data expertise with strong leadership and project management skills to drive successful migrations, integrations, and greenfield developments across the Azure ecosystem.
______________
Key Responsibilities
Delivery Leadership:
Own the technical delivery of Azure projects from Requirement through deployment.
Ensure adherence to Azure Well-Architected Framework, governance, and security standards.
Ability to build consensus and drive offshore global development teams to deliver build and QA.
Work with product owners and managers to track requirements, UAT closure, Communication, and Training.
Solution Architecture:
Review cloud designs and provide guidance to the Solution team.
Oversee integration with third-party systems, SaaS platforms (like Salesforce), and legacy on-prem applications.
Own data migration strategies; possess deep-dive knowledge of ETL/ELT activities (Azure Data Factory) and environment rollouts.
Provide direction on solutioning, technical delivery guidance, and identifying requirement gaps.
Team Management:
Lead and mentor cloud engineers, data engineers, business analysts, and quality assurance teams.
Foster collaboration between product owners, business stakeholders, and technical infrastructure teams.
Stakeholder Engagement:
Act as the primary technical point of contact for business and IT leadership regarding Azure initiatives.
Identify gaps in the translation of business requirements into technical cloud specifications.
Provide status on progress and monitor milestones and task delivery across multiple workstreams.
Quality Assurance & Compliance:
Implement robust testing strategies and ensure compliance with data privacy regulations (e.g., HIPAA, GDPR, or industry-specific environmental regs).
Work with QA management to align on schedule and execution of testing adhering to organizational goals.
Continuous Improvement:
Stay updated on Azure roadmaps, new services, and emerging cloud technologies.
Recommend enhancements to optimize cloud performance, cost (FinOps), and user experience.
______________
Required Skills & Qualifications
Experience:
12+ years in IT with at least 8 years focused on the Microsoft Azure ecosystem.
Proven track record of delivering complex Azure cloud migrations and integrations in enterprise environments.
Minimum of three large-scale Azure implementation experiences (e.g., Data Warehouse modernization, App Service migrations, or complex Integration Hubs).
Certifications:
Minimum 1 Azure certifications (e.g., Solutions Architect Expert, DevOps Engineer Expert, Data Engineer, Administrator, Security Engineer).
PMP certification is optional and good to have.
Scaled Agile certification is optional and good to have.
Technical Expertise:
Strong knowledge of Azure Resource Manager (ARM), Bicep/Terraform, Azure Functions, and Logic Apps.
Experience with Azure SQL, Synapse Analytics, Azure Data Factory, and CI/CD pipelines (Azure DevOps/GitHub Actions).
Experience with monitoring tools (Azure Monitor/Log Analytics) and security frameworks (Microsoft Entra ID).
Leadership & Communication:
Excellent stakeholder management and team leadership skills.
Strong problem-solving and decision-making abilities in high-pressure delivery environments.
______________
Preferred Qualifications
Experience with Agile/Scrum methodologies.
Familiarity with DevOps practices for Azure.
Exposure to hybrid-cloud environments and integrations with CRM platforms (Salesforce).
Mandatory: Four-year college degree in Computer Science or Engineering.
Kafka Integration Architect  - CE , Please look for nonlocals willing to relocate if no locals.
Architecture & Design
Define and own the enterprise event streaming architecture using Apache Kafka and related technologies (Kafka Connect, Kafka Streams, ksqlDB, Schema Registry)
Design scalable, fault-tolerant, and highly available data pipeline patterns for real-time and near-real-time use cases
Establish topic design standards, partitioning strategies, retention policies, and consumer group patterns
Evaluate and select Kafka deployment models (self-managed, Confluent Platform, MSK, Aiven, etc.)
Integration
Architect end-to-end integrations between Kafka and upstream/downstream systems (databases, REST APIs, legacy systems, cloud services)
Design and govern Kafka Connect pipelines using source/sink connectors
Define schema management strategies using Avro, Protobuf, or JSON Schema with Schema Registry
Lead integration with microservices, event-driven architectures, and CQRS/event sourcing patterns
Governance & Standards
Establish and enforce Kafka governance frameworks: naming conventions, access control (ACLs/RBAC), data classification, and topic lifecycle management
Define SLAs for message delivery, latency, throughput, and durability
Drive observability standards including monitoring, alerting, and tracing (Prometheus, Grafana, Datadog, OpenTelemetry)
Leadership & Collaboration
Serve as a technical advisor and escalation point for development teams implementing Kafka-based solutions
Conduct architecture reviews and approve designs that touch the event streaming platform
Mentor engineers and promote best practices across the organization
Collaborate with security, platform, and data engineering teams

data.public_job_desc:
DevOps Engineer
Location: Onshore – : Lenexa, KS; Lubbock, TX; Colorado Springs, CO; Purchase, NY
Contract Duration: 1 year (average)
Work Authorization: Must be authorized to work in the US (Green Card, Work Visa, EAD, etc.)


Role Summary:
As a DevOps Engineer, you will streamline the software development lifecycle by enhancing automation systems for software releases. You will ensure the integrity of CI/CD pipelines, fortify application deployment resilience, and advance monitoring capabilities. Collaborate with Senior DevOps Engineers and technical leads to deploy cutting-edge applications and optimize processes for efficiency, reliability, and scalability.
Responsibilities:
Design and build DevOps processes for large-scale applications (on-premise and cloud).
Manage build plans, assist developers, and oversee software configurations.
Ensure automated testing and continuous deployments.
Debug production outages and perform root-cause analysis.
Monitor production environments and maintain system stability.
Provide off-hour support for mission-critical applications.
Follow SDLC standards and compliance methodologies.
Optimize hardware, software, and personnel resources.
Address technical incidents per SLAs.
Integrate security into DevOps pipelines.
Maintain build, pipelines, ALM platforms, Azure deployments, and server support.
Deployments occur every 2 weeks.
Must-Have Skills:
Minimum 5 years DevOps experience.
Strong Azure experience (preferred over AWS/GCP).
.Net 8, Node JS, Delphi code for legacy systems.
Kubernetes, GitLab, Flux CD, GetOps.
CI/CD pipeline management.
SQL/Database Administration.
Security standards implementation.
Experience with automation tools (Ansible, Nginx).
REST APIs, SPAs, gRPC.
Application development tools (GIT, Visual Studio/Azure DevOps).
Monitoring tools for operational reporting.
Qualifications:
Bachelor’s degree in computer science, engineering, or related field.
Team player with solid communication and problem-solving skills.
Ability to manage multiple projects and work independently.

data.public_job_desc:
Senior SAP FSCD Business Analyst
Preferred  Locations: Charlotte, NC; Morristown, NJ; Purchase, NY; Conshohocken, PA
Acceptable Locations: Alpharetta, GA; Chicago, IL; Garden City, NY; New York City, NY; Denver, CO; Fargo, ND; Houston, TX; Lubbock, TX; Mt. Juliet, TN 

______________
Role Overview
Senior SAP FSCD Business Analyst, supporting SAP integration and expansion projects post-acquisition. This role focuses on SAP FSCD (Collections and Disbursements) and requires strong business analysis and finance expertise.
______________
Key Responsibilities
Gather, analyze, document, validate, and secure approval for complex business requirements across finance functions, with a focus on SAP FS-CD Collections and Disbursements.
Act as the primary liaison between business stakeholders and SAP development, configuration, and QA teams.
Lead planning, creation, and execution of comprehensive UAT strategies.
Maintain deep functional expertise in SAP FS-CD, including payments, broker reports, account maintenance, installment plans, dunning, autoclearing, and system integrations.
Support additional initiatives and responsibilities as assigned.
______________
Technical Qualifications
Minimum 7 years of experience implementing SAP financial applications, including the Collection and Disbursement Module (CD).
Minimum 5 years of experience in the insurance or financial services industry.
Strong business acumen and ability to translate complex business needs into effective SAP solutions.
Excellent written and verbal English communication skills.
______________
General Qualifications
Proven success leading cross-functional requirements workshops.
Ability to manage multiple priorities in fast-paced environments.
Experience presenting technical concepts to non-technical audiences.
Must-Have Skills
SAP FSCD OR strong SAP FI/GL experience
✓ SAP FI/GL knowledge
✓ Business analysis (requirements gathering, documentation, validation)
✓ UAT planning and execution
✓ Insurance or financial services industry experience
✓ Strong business acumen
✓ Excellent written and verbal English communication
______________
Nice-to-Have Skills
M&A integration experience
SAP FICO/FICA knowledge
Experience with global SAP expansion
Master’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, or Information Systems
Experience with QuickBooks to SAP migration

data.public_job_desc:
Job Title: GRC Engineer

Location: San Jose CA
Experience: 6–8 Years
Job Type: contract

Job Summary

We are seeking a hands-on GRC Engineer with strong technical expertise in Python, API development, and modern cloud-native architectures. The ideal candidate should have experience in building secure, scalable systems and working with compliance and governance frameworks in enterprise environments.

Key Responsibilities

  • Design and develop secure and scalable applications aligned with GRC (Governance, Risk, and Compliance) requirements.
  • Build and maintain APIs and backend services using modern development practices.
  • Work with microservices-based architecture and containerized environments.
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure security, compliance, and performance standards.
  • Support deployment and operations in cloud-native environments.

Required Skills

  • Strong hands-on experience in Python development
  • Experience in API development and integration
  • Knowledge of microservices architecture
  • Experience with Docker and Kubernetes
  • Strong understanding of secure system design
  • Solid experience with databases (SQL/NoSQL)
  • Understanding of GRC frameworks and compliance standards

Desirable Skills

  • Experience with Node.js (Digital stack)
  • Exposure to cloud platforms (AWS/Azure/GCP)
  • Experience in security, compliance, or audit-driven environments

Key Attributes

  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
  • Ability to design scalable and secure systems
  • Strong collaboration and communication skills
  • Experience working in Agile environments

Keywords

GRC Engineer | Python | API Development | Microservices | Kubernetes | Docker | Node.js | Compliance | Security Engineering | Cloud Native

data.public_job_desc:
Job Title: Senior Cloud Architect – VMware Cloud Foundation (VCF)

The Ideal Candidate Profile: The ideal candidate is a visionary technical leader who can confidently traverse the entire VMware Cloud Foundation (VCF) full-stack Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS) platform. This architect must seamlessly bridge the gap between traditional enterprise software and AI-enhanced modern applications, driving business value such as lower infrastructure costs, increased IT efficiency, and faster deployment times They must possess deep expertise in designing and deploying VCF Fleets, VCF Instances, Management Domains, and Workload Domains. Furthermore, they should be highly proficient and experienced in integrating core compute, storage, networking, security, and cloud automation in a manner valuable to customers.

Location: Remote

About the Role:

We are seeking a Senior Cloud Architect to lead the design, implementation, and optimization of our modern private cloud platform using VMware Cloud Foundation 9.x. You will be responsible for architecting a flexible, high-performing IT infrastructure that supports both traditional virtual machines (VMs) and modern containerized Kubernetes and AI/ML workloads. Your expertise will directly drive our business outcomes, targeting massive ROI, reduced unplanned downtime, and heightened developer productivity.

Experience Required:

Minimum 5 years of VCF 5.2+ hands-on experience at enterprise scale.

Required Certifications:

VCP-VCF-Architect

VCP VCF-Administrator (version 9)

Additional Preferred Certifications:

VCP-VCF Architect and Administrator (5.x)

TOGAF

Past VCAP / VCIX

Networking certification (CCNA, AVI, F5 (Load Balancing))

Key Responsibilities:

End-to-End VCF Architecture: Design and implement VCF Fleets and Instances across single-site, multi-site, and geographically dispersed Edge environments.

Infrastructure Design: Architect robust Workload Domains and vSphere Cluster models, selecting appropriate hardware, scaling solutions, and fault domains for enterprise workloads.

Advanced Networking & Security: Design Layer 2 to Layer 7 network stacks using NSX, implementing Virtual Private Clouds (VPCs), distributed load balancing, dynamic routing, and comprehensive security policies

Storage Architecture: Define storage models utilizing vSAN Express Storage Architecture (ESA), vSAN Original Storage Architecture (OSA), Fibre Channel, and NFS to meet stringent performance and availability requirements

Cloud Automation & Self-Service: Deploy and configure VCF Automation (formerly Aria Automation) to provide application teams with a self-service catalog for provisioning VMs, Kubernetes clusters, and databases within a secure, multi-tenant environment ? Automation: Ability to understand and code key languages such as Javascript, Powershell, Python, etc. pertinent to the VCF platform.

Fleet Management & Operations: Implement VCF Operations (formerly Aria Operations) for centralized logging, network visibility, FinOps (cost and capacity management), and proactive health monitoring across the entire cloud fleet

Modern Workload Enablement: Enable vSphere Supervisor to embed a Kubernetes control plane directly into ESXi hosts, delivering seamless vSphere Kubernetes Service (VKS) clusters for DevOps teams.

Resilience & Compliance: Architect Disaster Recovery (DR) and High Availability (HA) solutions using Stretched Clusters, VMware Live Recovery, and VCF Identity Broker for unified Single Sign-On (SSO) and compliance tracking.

Core Competencies & Requirements:

The VCF Core Stack: Expert-level mastery of VMware vSphere (ESXi and vCenter), vSAN, NSX, and SDDC Manager.

VCF Operations & Automation: Proven track record deploying VCF Operations and VCF Automation for fleet lifecycle management, password/certificate management, and orchestration

Blueprint Translation: Ability to translate complex business requirements into standard VCF Design Blueprints (e.g., Multi-Region with DR, Single Site with High Availability, or Locally Managed Edge configurations).

Networking Expertise: Deep understanding of network fabric models, BGP EVPN, Tier-0/Tier-1 gateways, and NSX Edge Cluster designs.

Workload Mobility: Experience utilizing VCF Operations HCX for secure, automated workload migration and interconnection across private clouds.

data.public_job_desc:
Principal AI Engineer

Locations: Austin [Hybrid]

LPL financial

 

 

Role Overview

We are seeking a highly hands-on AI Engineering leader with deep expertise in Generative AI, Agentic systems, and production-grade AI platforms.

This role is not a pure management role — the ideal candidate will actively design, build, and scale AI systems (RAG, agents, evaluation frameworks) while leading engineering initiatives and influencing platform strategy.

The candidate must demonstrate strong AI + AWS cloud expertise, with proven experience delivering enterprise-grade AI solutions in production environments.

Core Responsibilities

AI System Design & Development

  • Design and build production-grade GenAI systems, including: 
    • Multi-agent architectures 
    • Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines 
    • GraphRAG implementations 
    • Autonomous agent workflows and orchestration 
  • Develop and integrate AI agents with tools, APIs, and enterprise systems 
  • Implement MCP-based agent communication and tool-use frameworks 
  • Apply advanced prompt engineering techniques for reliability and performance 

Agentic AI & Evaluation

  • Build and deploy multi-agent orchestration systems 
  • Develop and implement: 
    • Agent evaluation frameworks 
    • RAG evaluation pipelines 
  • Measure and optimize: 
    • Output quality 
    • Hallucination rates 
    • Relevance and groundedness 
  • Continuously improve models through evaluation-driven iteration 

Engineering & Platform Development

  • Develop APIs and services using: 
    • Python (primary) 
    • .NET (preferred) 
  • Build scalable AI services with: 
    • REST APIs 
    • Microservices architecture 
  • Contribute to web-based AI applications using: 
    • Angular / TypeScript (preferred) 
  • Integrate AI systems into enterprise workflows and applications 

Cloud & Infrastructure (AWS Focus)

  • Design and deploy AI solutions on AWS, leveraging: 
    • Lambda, S3, EC2, EKS, Glue, SNS, SQS 
    • Kafka-based streaming architectures 
  • Build scalable and secure AI pipelines using cloud-native patterns 
  • Implement cost-efficient and high-performance AI workloads 

DevOps & CI/CD

  • Design and implement CI/CD pipelines using GitHub Actions 
  • Integrate AI workflows into CI/CD pipelines with strong AWS integration 
  • Ensure: 
    • Automated deployment 
    • Testing and validation of AI systems 
    • Continuous monitoring and iteration 

AI Development Tooling

  • Leverage modern AI development tools and ecosystems, including: 
    • Claude (Claude API / Claude Code) 
    • Cursor AI (AI-assisted development workflows) 
  • Build and optimize developer workflows using AI-assisted coding tools 

Required Qualifications

  • 10+ years of overall engineering experience 
  • 5+ years of hands-on AI/ML / GenAI development in production environments 
  • Strong experience building: 
    • AI agents (minimum 2+ implementations) 
    • GraphRAG systems (minimum 2+ implementations) 
    • MCP-based integrations (minimum 1+) 
  • Proven expertise in: 
    • Multi-agent orchestration 
    • RAG pipelines 
    • Agent and RAG evaluation frameworks 
  • Strong programming skills in: 
    • Python (must-have) 
  • Experience with: 
    • API development and system integration 
  • Strong experience with: 
    • AWS cloud platform (must-have) 

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience with: 
    • .NET / C# development 
    • Terraform (Infrastructure as Code) 
  • Experience building: 
    • Web applications using Angular / TypeScript 
  • Familiarity with: 
    • Kafka-based streaming systems 
  • Exposure to: 
    • Advanced AI orchestration frameworks (LangChain, LangGraph, etc.)

data.position_title: SAP APO Consultant (DP / SNP / PPDS / GATP)
data.public_job_desc:
Job Title: SAP APO Consultant (DP / SNP / PPDS / GATP)

Location: Raritan, NJ
Duration: Contract
Experience Required: 8+ Years

Job Summary

We are seeking an experienced SAP APO Consultant with strong expertise across Demand Planning (DP), Supply Network Planning (SNP), Production Planning and Detailed Scheduling (PP/DS), and Global Available-to-Promise (GATP). The ideal candidate will lead end-to-end SAP APO implementations and support engagements, working closely with business stakeholders to design, configure, optimize, and stabilize supply chain planning solutions integrated with SAP ERP.

Key Responsibilities

SAP APO Implementation & Support

  • Lead end-to-end SAP APO implementations and support activities across DP, SNP, PP/DS, and GATP modules.
  • Participate in requirement gathering, solution design, configuration, testing (UT/SIT/UAT), cutover, deployment, and hypercare support.
  • Conduct business workshops to understand planning and fulfillment processes and document AS-IS and TO-BE business flows.
  • Translate business requirements into functional specifications, configuration documents, and solution designs.

APO–ERP Integration & CIF

  • Build, monitor, and stabilize APO–ERP integration using CIF.
  • Create and maintain integration models and troubleshoot qRFC queue issues.
  • Perform data reconciliation between APO and ERP systems.
  • Ensure smooth transfer of master and transactional data between systems.

Master Data Management

  • Ensure APO master data readiness and governance including:
    • Products
    • Locations
    • Resources
    • Calendars
    • Planning horizons
    • Transportation lanes
    • Quota arrangements
    • PDS/PPM structures
  • Maintain alignment between APO and ERP master data objects.

SAP APO Demand Planning (DP)

  • Configure planning areas, planning levels, key figures, versions, and time series settings.
  • Implement and tune statistical forecasting models and forecasting parameters.
  • Manage forecast outliers, intermittency, and exception-based planning using macros.
  • Define and monitor forecast accuracy KPIs such as Bias and MAPE.
  • Support S&OP and consensus demand planning processes.

SAP APO Supply Network Planning (SNP)

  • Configure SNP for multi-echelon supply chain planning including sourcing, lot sizing, lead times, safety stock, and constraints.
  • Execute and optimize SNP heuristics and optimizer planning runs.
  • Analyze planning results and improve planning logic for inventory, service levels, and capacity utilization.
  • Implement SNP deployment and replenishment processes.
  • Configure Transport Load Builder (TLB) where applicable.

SAP APO PP/DS

  • Configure PP/DS for finite scheduling and detailed sequencing.
  • Maintain capacities, modes, setup matrices, pegging strategies, and sequence-dependent setups.
  • Ensure accurate PDS/PPM integration with ERP BOMs, routings, and production versions.
  • Support planners using DS Planning Board and PP/DS heuristics to resolve scheduling bottlenecks and improve production efficiency.

SAP GATP & Backorder Processing

  • Configure GATP check strategies and Rules-Based ATP (RBA).
  • Implement and support Backorder Processing (BOP) based on customer priorities and allocation policies.
  • Configure product allocation and substitution logic for constrained supply scenarios.
  • Improve order confirmation accuracy and supply allocation effectiveness.

Enhancements & Support

  • Develop functional specifications for custom enhancements and interfaces.
  • Coordinate with ABAP and technical teams for development and testing activities.
  • Perform root-cause analysis for planning and integration issues including:
    • CIF failures
    • Incorrect pegging
    • Missing receipts
    • Planning inconsistencies
    • Master data gaps
  • Create operational runbooks, monitoring procedures, and job schedules.
  • Deliver end-user training, SOPs, and knowledge transfer sessions.

Required Skills

  • Strong hands-on experience with SAP APO DP, SNP, PP/DS, and GATP.
  • Expertise in CIF integration and APO–ERP master data synchronization.
  • Experience with forecasting models, supply planning, finite scheduling, and ATP processes.
  • Strong understanding of supply chain planning and manufacturing business processes.
  • Experience in APO planning heuristics, optimizer, deployment, and scheduling boards.
  • Ability to troubleshoot integration and planning issues independently.
  • Experience preparing functional specifications and supporting custom developments.
  • Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience with SAP ECC and/or SAP S/4HANA integration.
  • Exposure to SAP IBP migration or coexistence scenarios is a plus.
  • SAP APO certification is preferred.
  • Experience in manufacturing, retail, consumer goods, or supply chain industries is highly desirable.

data.public_job_desc:
Job Title: Data Engineer III
Duration: 12+ Months Contract
Work Location: Onsite (Flexible Hybrid – Expected to work most of the week onsite)
Interview Process: 60-Minute Zoom Video Interview

Job Summary

We are seeking a hands-on Data Engineer III to design, build, and optimize scalable data pipelines, automation workflows, and BI solutions. The ideal candidate will have strong SQL expertise, ETL development experience, geospatial/GIS knowledge, and exposure to frontend/backend application development.

This role focuses on integrating data from multiple enterprise systems, ensuring high-quality data delivery, and enabling actionable business insights through automation and reporting solutions.

Key Responsibilities

  • Develop and maintain ETL/ELT pipelines integrating data from diverse systems and platforms
  • Write, optimize, and maintain advanced SQL queries, stored procedures, views, and database objects
  • Design scalable and high-performance data pipeline architectures
  • Build automated workflows using Python, SQL, Alteryx, Databricks, and related technologies
  • Develop and support BI dashboards using Power BI, Tableau, or Spotfire
  • Work closely with business and technical teams to translate requirements into technical solutions
  • Support and maintain full-stack web applications using modern frontend frameworks and backend services
  • Manage multiple priorities and deliverables independently in a fast-paced environment

Required Skills

  • Strong SQL expertise including complex query writing, optimization, and performance tuning
  • Experience with GIS/Spatial Data technologies such as ArcGIS, Spatial SQL, Python/ArcPy
  • Strong ETL development experience across multiple platforms
  • Intermediate frontend and backend software development knowledge
  • Experience designing and optimizing scalable data pipelines and architectures
  • Strong communication and collaboration skills
  • Ability to work independently with minimal supervision

Preferred Skills

  • Experience with Databricks, Azure Data Factory, or similar cloud data platforms
  • Knowledge of data governance, data quality, and lifecycle management
  • Experience with BI/reporting tools including Power BI, Tableau, or Spotfire
  • Additional programming experience with JavaScript or related technologies

Top 3 Required Skills

  1. Strong SQL Knowledge
  2. Intermediate Software Development Experience (Frontend & Backend)
  3. Intermediate Geospatial/GIS Skills (Spatial Data Formats, Coordinate Systems, ArcGIS)

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Engineering, or related field preferred
  • Proven experience in Data Engineering, ETL Development, and Data Integration projects
  • Experience working in enterprise-scale data environments preferred

data.public_job_desc:
Job Title: Technical Program Manager (TPM)

Location: Costa Mesa
Experience Level: [8–12+ years]

 

Role Overview

We are looking for an experienced Technical Program Manager to lead complex, cross-functional programs across our engineering and platform teams. This role requires a strong blend of technical acumen, program execution rigor, and stakeholder management to drive large-scale initiatives from inception to delivery.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead end-to-end delivery of complex technical programs across multiple teams (engineering, data, platform, and product).
  • Define program scope, goals, milestones, and success metrics aligned with business objectives.
  • Drive program planning, execution, risk management, and timely delivery.
  • Collaborate with architects and engineering teams to ensure scalable and efficient technical solutions.
  • Establish and enforce best practices for program governance, reporting, and communication.
  • Identify and proactively mitigate risks, dependencies, and bottlenecks.
  • Manage stakeholder expectations across senior leadership, product, and engineering teams.
  • Drive continuous improvement in delivery processes, including Agile/PI planning and execution.
  • Track and report program status, KPIs, and outcomes.

Required Qualifications

  • 10+ years of experience in Technical Program Management or related roles.
  • Strong technical background with experience in cloud platforms (preferably AWS), distributed systems, or data platforms.
  • Proven track record of delivering large-scale, complex programs in enterprise environments.
  • Strong understanding of Agile methodologies and program management frameworks.
  • Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.
  • Ability to operate effectively in a fast-paced, ambiguous environment.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience in financial services, data platforms, or regulated industries.
  • Familiarity with cloud cost optimization and operational excellence practices.
  • Experience working with cross-geography teams and offshore/onshore models.
  • PMP, Scrum Master, or equivalent certifications.

Key Skills

  • Program & Portfolio Management
  • Technical Architecture Understanding
  • Risk & Dependency Management
  • Stakeholder Communication
  • Agile / SAFe / PI Planning
  • Data & Cloud (AWS) Knowledge

data.requisition_description:
Job Title: Technical Program Manager (TPM)

Location: Costa Mesa
Experience Level: [8–12+ years]

 

Role Overview

We are looking for an experienced Technical Program Manager to lead complex, cross-functional programs across our engineering and platform teams. This role requires a strong blend of technical acumen, program execution rigor, and stakeholder management to drive large-scale initiatives from inception to delivery.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead end-to-end delivery of complex technical programs across multiple teams (engineering, data, platform, and product).
  • Define program scope, goals, milestones, and success metrics aligned with business objectives.
  • Drive program planning, execution, risk management, and timely delivery.
  • Collaborate with architects and engineering teams to ensure scalable and efficient technical solutions.
  • Establish and enforce best practices for program governance, reporting, and communication.
  • Identify and proactively mitigate risks, dependencies, and bottlenecks.
  • Manage stakeholder expectations across senior leadership, product, and engineering teams.
  • Drive continuous improvement in delivery processes, including Agile/PI planning and execution.
  • Track and report program status, KPIs, and outcomes.

Required Qualifications

  • 10+ years of experience in Technical Program Management or related roles.
  • Strong technical background with experience in cloud platforms (preferably AWS), distributed systems, or data platforms.
  • Proven track record of delivering large-scale, complex programs in enterprise environments.
  • Strong understanding of Agile methodologies and program management frameworks.
  • Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.
  • Ability to operate effectively in a fast-paced, ambiguous environment.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience in financial services, data platforms, or regulated industries.
  • Familiarity with cloud cost optimization and operational excellence practices.
  • Experience working with cross-geography teams and offshore/onshore models.
  • PMP, Scrum Master, or equivalent certifications.

Key Skills

  • Program & Portfolio Management
  • Technical Architecture Understanding
  • Risk & Dependency Management
  • Stakeholder Communication
  • Agile / SAFe / PI Planning
  • Data & Cloud (AWS) Knowledge

ALL CAPS, NO SPACES B/T UNDERSCORES PTN_US_GBAMSREQID_
Candidate BeelineID i.e. PTN_US_9999999_SKIPJOHNSON0413
MSP Owner: Thomas Hodges
Targeted Bill Rate- $65-$75
REQUIREMENT_CITY - Alpharetta, GA - Need to work from office 3 Days a week - Will be Face2Face Round of Interview
REQUIREMENT_ID-10780881
Role Name - AI SRE

ROLE_DESCRIPTION -

Skill Set - Expertise in UNIX + LINUX Administration + AWS/ AZURE Cloud monitoring + Terraform/ Ansible + Prometheus/ Grafana observability experience).
Work Location - Alpharetta
Experience required for role - 6+ years
• Production experience in SRE / Infrastructure / ops for large-scale systems
• Strong programming/scripting skills (Python, Go, Java, or equivalent)
• Deep experience with containerization (Docker), orchestration (Kubernetes, etc.)
• Infrastructure-as-code (Terraform, Helm, CloudFormation, Ansible, etc.)
• Familiarity with GPU / AI compute clusters, high-performance data storage, and distributed architectures
• Experience with monitoring / observability / logging / alerting tools (Prometheus, Grafana, ELK / EFK, Datadog, etc.)
• Networking & systems engineering knowledge (TCP/IP, DNS, routing, load balancing, distributed storage)
• Solid experience in capacity planning, performance tuning, scaling, and incident response
• Demonstrated ability to lead RCAs, deploy fixes, and drive reliability improvements
• Experience in regulated environments (financial services, compliance, audit, security) is a strong plus
• Excellent communication, documentation, and cross-team collaboration skills
• Proven track record of reducing operational toil via automation
Experience: 6+ years of experience as a Site Reliability Engineer or in a similar role, with hands-on experience in supporting IaaS platforms with networking and system engineering knowledge.
• Operate, monitor, and maintain the infrastructure supporting GenAI applications (training, inference, feature store, data ingestion, model serving)
• Design and build automation for core platform capabilities, reducing manual toil
• Develop and maintain infrastructure-as-code (IaC) for provisioning and managing compute, storage, network, GPU clusters, Kubernetes / container orchestration, etc.
• Establish, monitor, and enforce SLOs/SLIs/SLAs, error budgets, alerting, and dashboards
• Lead incident response, root cause analysis (RCA), postmortems, and systemic remediation
• Perform capacity planning, scaling strategies, workload scheduling, and resource forecasting
• Optimize cost vs. performance tradeoffs in large-scale compute environments
• Harden systems for security, compliance, auditability, and data governance
• Collaborate across teams (cloud engineers, data engineers, infrastructure, security) to ensure safe deployment, rollout, rollback, and integration of new systems
• Define disaster recovery (DR) strategies, backup/restore practices, fault tolerance mechanisms
• Maintain runbooks, operational playbooks, documentation, and training materials
• Participate in on-call rotations and respond to production incidents 24/7 as needed
• Continuously evaluate and integrate new tools, frameworks, or technologies to enhance platform reliability


Skills: Digital : Python~Digital : Docker~Digital : Kubernetes~Digital : Site Reliability Engineering (SRE)
Experience Required: 6-8, Project Code :
